skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Route still-image animation requests to the right RunComfy image-to-video model and prepare the matching CLI inputs.
skill.summary: Animate still images with RunComfy model routing and prompt guidance.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns in SKILL.md. Review found that most command and cryptography alerts are Markdown inline-code or CLI examples, but the skill intentionally sends prompts and user media URLs to RunComfy through the local CLI. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Submits User Content
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run the local RunComfy CLI, which posts model requests and downloads generated outputs. This is expected behavior, but user prompts and media references leave the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly says the CLI posts to the Model API and downloads RunComfy URLs. This confirms external processing, but it is disclosed and aligned with the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Media URLs Are Sent To A Third-Party Service
medium_findings.description: Example inputs include image, video, and audio URLs that are processed by RunComfy model servers. Users should not provide private or sensitive media unless they accept that remote processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples and privacy section identify remote media URLs and external RunComfy endpoints. The risk is data exposure, not evidence of exfiltration beyond the documented service.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Cryptography Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many reported Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts are caused by Markdown inline code, model field names, shell examples, and documentation text. No Ruby code or weak cryptographic operation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples and prose, not executable Ruby or cryptographic code. This strongly supports dismissal of those static categories.

low_findings.title: Local Token File Is Documented
low_findings.description: The skill notes that RunComfy login stores an API token under the user's config directory with owner-only permissions. This is standard credential storage but should be disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path is explicitly documented. The text also states owner-only permissions, reducing severity while preserving a filesystem and credential-handling concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Invocation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to invoke `runcomfy run` with JSON input and an output directory. This is operationally necessary, but agents should avoid interpolating untrusted text into shell syntax.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The snippets are shell examples with JSON arguments. The security section claims prompt content is passed as JSON, so the risk is controlled but still worth flagging.

content.value_statement: Choosing an image-to-video model can waste time when each model has different strengths. This skill routes the request to a RunComfy model and prepares focused prompts and CLI inputs.
actual_capabilities: Routes general image animation requests to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V.
Routes custom voiceover lip-sync requests to Wan 2.7 with audio_url.
Routes multi-modal image, video, and audio reference requests to Seedance 2.0 Pro.
Provides documented field requirements for image, audio, video, duration, resolution, and aspect ratio.
Shows RunComfy CLI commands for each supported model route.
Includes prompt guidance for motion, identity preservation, lighting, and reference numbering.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and valid authentication.
Processes prompts and media URLs through RunComfy remote model APIs.
Each route inherits model limits for duration, resolution, aspect ratio, and file size.
Does not combine multiple model routes into one call or stitch videos.

use_cases.title: Create portrait motion clips
use_cases.description: Animate a still portrait with stable identity, gentle camera movement, and simple motion direction.

use_cases.title: Produce product reveal videos
use_cases.description: Turn product images into short reveal clips with controlled camera motion and preserved packaging details.

use_cases.title: Generate localized spokesperson clips
use_cases.description: Use the same visual concept with different audio URLs for language variants and lip-sync tests.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a still image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a general image-to-video clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image with natural motion. Keep the subject stable, add a slow camera drift, and use soft realistic lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Create a product reveal
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for brand or product motion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product image as a clean reveal. Preserve the packaging text, keep geometry stable, and use one smooth orbit motion.

prompt_templates.title: Make a lip-sync clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with a custom voiceover URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a vertical talking-head video using this voiceover URL. Use a medium close-up, locked camera, warm key light, and matched duration.

prompt_templates.title: Combine reference media
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced multi-modal request with image, video, and audio references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image 1 for the subject, video 1 for scene movement, and audio 1 for voice tone. Make an 8 second cinematic clip.

output_examples.input: A portrait photo with a request for subtle movement.
output_examples.output: The skill selects HappyHorse 1.0 I2V and prepares a motion-focused prompt with identity-stable facial features.

output_examples.input: A spokesperson concept with an MP3 voiceover URL.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Wan 2.7, sets audio_url, matches duration to the track, and frames the talking-head shot.

output_examples.input: A subject image, cafe reference video, and voice reference audio.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Seedance 2.0 Pro and numbers each reference so the model can map subject, scene, and voice cues.

best_practices: Use one primary motion beat per short clip.
State what must remain stable, such as identity, packaging, or background geometry.
Match audio duration and media specs to the selected model before running the CLI.
anti_patterns: Do not send private media URLs unless remote RunComfy processing is acceptable.
Do not mix unrelated visual styles across image and video references.
Do not request multiple model routes in one call when stitching is required.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes image animation requests to a suitable RunComfy model and provides the matching CLI input pattern.

faq.question: Which model is used by default?
faq.answer: The skill uses HappyHorse 1.0 I2V for general portrait, product, and still-image animation requests.

faq.question: Can it use a custom voiceover?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes custom voiceover lip-sync requests to Wan 2.7 with an audio_url field.

faq.question: Can it combine image, video, and audio references?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Seedance 2.0 Pro when the request includes image, video, and audio reference media.

faq.question: Does it run fully locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI, but model processing happens through RunComfy remote APIs.

faq.question: What should users prepare first?
faq.answer: Users need the RunComfy CLI, authentication, source media URLs, and a clear motion or lip-sync goal.
